scope: New Zealand-admitted barristers, solicitors, King's Counsel, retired judges and legal academics who have appeared as named counsel in a reported PA decision, published in the field, sat on relevant working groups / Family Court reviews / Family Violence Death Review Committee, or publicly stated a sourced stance. Stance is sourced, not inferred. Post-2019 Te Korowai Ture-a-Whānau, Care of Children Act 2004 and Family Violence Act 2018 doctrinal context applies throughout.

stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA as a legitimate, litigable phenomenon worth pursuing in family court
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA is misused in family court, especially against DV / family-violence survivors
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both family-violence and alienating-behaviour frames; aligned with post-2019 Te Korowai Ture-a-Whānau child-centred + safety-primary line

practitioners.name: Lee James
practitioners.firm: Nelson, NZ (Nelson-based solicitor and law firm director; specific firm name not publicly confirmed on a single canonical site)
bar_admissions: NZ (barrister and solicitor of the High Court of New Zealand; practising since 1996 per Otago / NZ Herald coverage)
named_cases: Lee A. James, Parental Alienation: The New Zealand Approach (LLM thesis, University of Otago, April 2018) — https://ourarchive.otago.ac.nz/esploro/outputs/graduate/Parental-Alienation-The-New-Zealand-Approach/9926480055701891
Subsequent publication in New Zealand Family Law Journal (2018)
RNZ Afternoons interview 2018 — https://www.rnz.co.nz/national/programmes/afternoons/audio/2018653156/parental-alienation-lee-james-calls-for-greater-family-court-therapy-powers
NZ Herald coverage — https://www.nzherald.co.nz/nz/family-court-not-equipped-to-deal-with-parental-alienation-otago-university-study-says/MUU6G2M2UJX736SCMHRBXAXIQE/
practitioners.primary_contact: Via NZ Law Society register https://www.lawsociety.org.nz/for-the-community/find-lawyer-and-organisation ; via University of Otago Faculty of Law (thesis supervisor route)
practitioners.hook: The single most prominent recognition-camp NZ-lawyer voice — an LLM thesis explicitly distinguishing alienation from justified estrangement, published in the NZ Family Law Journal and amplified by the University of Otago press office.
practitioners.notes: Specific firm name not publicly confirmed at time of writing.

practitioners.name: Emeritus Professor Mark Henaghan FRSNZ
practitioners.firm: Faculty of Law, University of Auckland (current); formerly Dean, Faculty of Law, University of Otago (to April 2018)
bar_admissions: NZ practising-certificate status as litigator not publicly confirmed; primary role academic. FRSNZ.
named_cases: Co-author/editor of Family Law in New Zealand (Henaghan & Atkin), multiple editions
Supervisor of Lee A. James, Parental Alienation: The New Zealand Approach (LLM, Otago, 2018)
Cambridge Family Law Centre fellow — https://www.family.law.cam.ac.uk/professor-mark-henaghan

Quoted: parental alienation cases 'provide the biggest headache and challenge for the Family Court'; endorses early intervention so 'reunification processes [can] be put in place'; explicit on alienation / justified-estrangement distinction
practitioners.primary_contact: Via Auckland Faculty of Law profile https://www.auckland.ac.nz/en/law/study-with-us/study-options/meet-academic-staff/mark-henaghan.html
practitioners.hook: The most senior NZ family-law academic publicly aligned with a recognition-framed approach to PA — co-author of the standard NZ family-law text and academic supervisor of the only full-length NZ academic monograph proposing PA-aware reform.

practitioners.name: Jill Goldson
bar_admissions: Not a lawyer — family mediator and child-inclusive practitioner; postgraduate qualification in Parental Alienation Studies (Institute of Family Therapy, Malta, 2022)
named_cases: The Goldson Model — child-inclusive separation mediation framework adopted by NZ government for 'Bridging the Gap' online programme
Post-graduate qualification in Parental Alienation Studies (Institute of Family Therapy, Malta, 2022)
Public presentations index https://thefamilymatterscentre.co.nz/presentations/
Self-described: 'I help separated parents and the professionals who work with them, to understand the benefits of child inclusive mediation and how to manage issues of parental alienation'
practitioners.hook: Practitioner whose model has been adopted at NZ government level for out-of-court family mediation — every recognition-camp NZ lawyer who refers families to mediation will intersect with the Goldson framework.
practitioners.notes: Listed in lawyers directory as the named practitioner whose framework is most likely to be referenced by lawyers in Family Court alienation matters; therapist-side cross-reference belongs in a future NZ therapists directory.

practitioners.name: Darren Mort
practitioners.firm: Independent Family Law Bar, Melbourne (ANZPAC cross-jurisdictional speaker)
named_cases: ANZPAC 2025 keynote on Parental Alienation
See /practitioners-au-lawyers.md for full entry
practitioners.hook: Cross-jurisdictional ANZPAC reference; AU primary entry.

practitioners.name: Brian Ludmer
named_cases: Recurring ANZPAC speaker (AU/NZ Parental Alienation Conference)
See /practitioners-ca-lawyers.md for full entry
practitioners.hook: Cross-jurisdictional ANZPAC reference; CA primary entry.

practitioners.name: Associate Professor Carrie Leonetti
practitioners.firm: Auckland Law School / Te Wānanga o Te Ture, University of Auckland
bar_admissions: NZ practising-certificate status not publicly confirmed; primary role academic
named_cases: Leonetti, 'Endangered by Junk Science: How the New Zealand Family Court's Admission of Unreliable Expert Evidence Places Children at Risk' (2022) 43 Children's Legal Rights Law Journal
Leonetti, 'Sub Silentio Alienation' (2023) Washburn Law Review
Leonetti, 'Combatting a Dangerous American Export: the Need for Professional Regulation of Psychologists in the New Zealand Family Court' (2023) UCLA Pacific Basin Law Journal

Leonetti, 'Disbelieving children's disclosures and disregarding children's views' (2025) International Journal of Law, Policy and the Family — https://academic.oup.com/lawfam/article/39/1/ebaf008/8085137
Stuff / University of Auckland opinion piece (15 January 2025) on UK FJC challenge
Newsroom 14 November 2023 — https://newsroom.co.nz/2023/11/14/how-discredited-parental-alienation-syndrome-is-being-used-to-cover-up-abuse/
University of Auckland 'pseudoscience of parental alienation syndrome' (15 November 2023) — https://www.auckland.ac.nz/en/news/2023/11/15/the-pseudoscience-of--parental-alienation-syndrome--.html
Law Commission submission — https://www.lawcom.govt.nz/assets/Submissions/Evidence-Act-third-review/NZLC-IP50-Submission-15-Carrie-Leonetti-Proactive-release.pdf
practitioners.primary_contact: Via Auckland Faculty of Law staff page (University of Auckland directory)
practitioners.hook: The single most prolific NZ-based academic critic of PA in the NZ Family Court — three peer-reviewed law-journal articles, an Oxford journal article, a Law Commission submission, and a University of Auckland-amplified op-ed line.

practitioners.name: Professor Julia Tolmie
practitioners.firm: Faculty of Law, University of Auckland
bar_admissions: NZ practising-certificate status not publicly confirmed; primary role academic. Previously University of Sydney Faculty of Law 1989-1999.
named_cases: Chair, NZ Family Violence Death Review Committee Dec 2011-2016; deputy chair 2017
FVDRC Fifth Report (2016) — https://www.hqsc.govt.nz/assets/Our-work/Mortality-review-committee/FVDRC/Publications-resources/FVDRC-5th-report-Feb-2016-v2.pdf
Member, NZ Government Expert Advisory Group on Family Violence (2013)
Chair, 20 March 2018 University of Auckland seminar 'Parental alienation and family courts: a conversation on research and practice'
Published scholarship critiquing 'hostile and alienating mother' framings (interview study of 21 women)
practitioners.primary_contact: Via Auckland Faculty of Law staff page https://www.auckland.ac.nz/en/law/our-research/meet-our-researchers/julia-tolmie.html
practitioners.hook: The most institutionally weighted NZ critique voice — five-year Chair of the statutory Family Violence Death Review Committee. FVDRC reports are the citable doctrinal-history reference for the 'PAS as unconscious bias' reading of NZ Family Court decision-making.

practitioners.name: Catriona MacLennan
practitioners.firm: Independent / barrister sole; founder Wheels for Women; co-founder Ngā Ture Kaitiaki ki Waikato Community Law Centre
bar_admissions: NZ (14 years legal practice in South Auckland in family and domestic violence law per Vine seminar bio; specific call year not publicly confirmed)
named_cases: Newsroom column 'Family Court using discredited US theory' — https://www.newsroom.co.nz/family-court-using-discredited-us-theory
Newsroom 'Family Court's handling of violence under scrutiny' — https://www.newsroom.co.nz/family-court-under-international-scrutiny

Panel speaker, 'Parental alienation and family courts' seminar 20 March 2018 — https://vine.org.nz/webinars-seminars-and-conferences/parental-alienation-and-family-courts-a-conversation-on-research-and-practice
Founder, Wheels for Women (cars for DV survivors)
Co-founder, Ngā Ture Kaitiaki ki Waikato Community Law Centre

Quoted: 'we don't need to change the law, we just need to apply the Domestic Violence Act properly and stop using situational violence and parental alienation – constructs which are completely artificial'
practitioners.hook: The frontline practising-barrister voice in the NZ critique camp — bridges academic critique (Leonetti / Tolmie) to actual NZ case-level commentary in mainstream press.

practitioners.name: Associate Professor Ruth Busch (retired)
practitioners.firm: University of Waikato Te Piringa Faculty of Law (retired 2018 after 23 years); two free family-law clinics in retirement
bar_admissions: NZ (admitted to NZ practice post-1982 arrival from US; specific call year not publicly confirmed)
named_cases: Author of the report underlying the Domestic Violence Act 1995 (NZ)
Bristol Ministerial Inquiry contribution → family-violence provisions of Care of Children Act 2004
Co-founder, Hamilton Abuse Intervention Project (HAIP)
23 years at Waikato Te Piringa Faculty of Law; 2018 retirement
Quoted on retirement: 'domestic violence and its consequences continued to be minimised by the judiciary and other family law decision-makers'
practitioners.primary_contact: Retired; outreach via Waikato Te Piringa alumni / clinic networks. Direct contact not publicly confirmed.
practitioners.hook: The named architect of the legislative regime that the critique camp argues NZ Family Court has failed to apply properly when accepting PA framings.

practitioners.name: The Backbone Collective (Deborah Mackenzie, Ruth Herbert, Dr Tania Domett — co-founders)
practitioners.firm: The Backbone Collective (independent NZ DV-survivor response-system watchdog, founded 2017)
bar_admissions: Co-founders are not lawyers — policy / advocacy / research specialists (Mackenzie MA(Hons); Herbert and Domett DV-sector specialists)
named_cases: Blog 'WHY DOES NZ LAW SCHOOL SUPPORT A DISCREDITED THEORY DESPITE WARNINGS FROM PSYCHOLOGY EXPERTS?' 15 July 2018
Submission to the Review into the Family Court 2014 Reforms (Te Korowai Ture-a-Whānau) — https://www.justice.govt.nz/assets/Family-Court-rewrite-submission-Backbone-Collective.pdf
All Eyes on the Family Court watchdog report (2017)
Children's-experiences-of-the-Family-Court report — https://www.vine.org.nz/news/backbone-collective-releases-report-on-childrens-experiences-of-the-family-court
Supporting Children in Court Bill submission
Calls for a Royal Commission of Inquiry into the Family Court (per UN Human Rights Committee referral)
Over half of women surveyed reported being accused of parental alienation
practitioners.hook: The single most-cited NZ critique-camp organisation in mainstream press and academic literature; July 2018 critique of the Lee James LLM thesis is the load-bearing public counter-text to the recognition-camp NZ literature.
practitioners.notes: Listed in lawyers directory because the Collective's submissions are named in NZLS / Vine / Newsroom commentary on PA.

practitioners.name: National Collective of Independent Women's Refuges (NCIWR) — Women's Refuge NZ
practitioners.firm: NCIWR — New Zealand's largest domestic violence organisation
named_cases: NCIWR Submission to the Family Court Review (Te Korowai Ture-a-Whānau panel) — https://womensrefuge.org.nz/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/Submission-to-the-Family-Court-Review.docx
NCIWR CEDAW alternate report 2018 — https://womensrefuge.org.nz/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/NCIWR-CEDAW-alternate-report-2018-final.pdf
Rethinking 'risk': using risk assessment data in family violence and safety work
Serves c.26,699 clients annually (47% women, 53% children)
CEDAW alternate report (2018): 'unconscious bias is especially evident in judges' use of Parental Alienation Syndrome'
practitioners.hook: National peak DV-services body whose CEDAW alternate report puts PAS-related unconscious bias on the international human-rights record.

practitioners.name: The Hon. Peter Boshier (former Principal Family Court Judge; former Chief Ombudsman)
bar_admissions: NZ (practised in Wellington pre-1988); appointed District Court Judge 1988; Principal Family Court Judge 2004-2012; Chief Ombudsman 2015-2025
named_cases: 8 years as Principal Family Court Judge (2004-2012)
'Challenges Facing the Family Court' (2008) NZLII speech — https://www.nzlii.org/nz/journals/NZJlPubIntLaw/2008/10.pdf
Member, Family Violence Taskforce
Chief Ombudsman 2015-2025
practitioners.primary_contact: Retired from Ombudsman role March 2025; direct contact via personal site https://peterboshier.com/
practitioners.hook: Former Principal Family Court Judge whose era underpins the modern NZ doctrinal context; the named former-judge any responsible NZ doctrinal write-up has to cite.
practitioners.notes: Specific public stance on PA not publicly confirmed.

practitioners.name: Anita Chan KC
practitioners.firm: Princes Chambers, 3rd Floor, 155 Princes Street, Dunedin
bar_admissions: NZ (Otago LLB 1984; admitted barrister and solicitor of the High Court; joined Barristers Chambers Dunedin late 1997; helped establish Princes Chambers; appointed Queen's Counsel 2014 — first person of Asian descent appointed QC in NZ)
named_cases: Former Vice President, International Academy of Family Lawyers (IAFL)
Former Chair, New Zealand Family Law Section (NZLS)
Court-appointed Lawyer for the Child in complex matters
Doyle's Guide leading senior family-law barrister (2024)
Practice: complex relationship-property and children's cases including international relocation and Hague Convention abduction
practitioners.primary_contact: +64 3 477 8781 ; Princes Chambers, 3rd Floor, 155 Princes Street, Dunedin (referral via instructing solicitor only)
practitioners.hook: Single highest-credential NZ family-law KC in regular Hague Convention / international relocation practice — natural NZ outreach counterpart to AU and UK KC-level voices.
practitioners.notes: Explicit PA stance not publicly confirmed; classed middle on basis of practice profile.

practitioners.name: Lynda Kearns KC
practitioners.firm: Bastion Chambers, Level 3, 70 Shortland Street, Auckland
bar_admissions: NZ (admitted barrister and solicitor of the High Court 1981; King's Counsel 2021; senior counsel for child status since 1993)
named_cases: Senior counsel for child since 1993 (NZ — one of the first cohort)
Chair, NZ Bar Association Family, Trusts and Estates Committee (2024-)
IAFL fellow (one of six NZ members)
Quoted: 'particular expertise in cases involving domestic violence, custody and child protection, relocation and Hague convention matters'
Routinely appointed by the Court to act for children in difficult or longstanding cases and as counsel to assist / amicus
practitioners.hook: Most experienced court-appointed lawyer-for-the-child KC in NZ — the practitioner most likely to be amicus or counsel-to-assist in any apex-level NZ PA appellate case.
practitioners.notes: Specific public PA stance not publicly confirmed; classed middle on basis of court-appointed lawyer-for-child practice profile.

practitioners.name: Lady Deborah Chambers KC
bar_admissions: NZ (joined Bankside Chambers 2007 and appointed Queen's Counsel the same year; specific year of admission not publicly confirmed on chambers profile)
named_cases: 2023 Supreme Court argument on fiduciary obligation of abusive parent re trust transfers (with Josie Beverwijk and Isaac Hikaka of Mills Lane Chambers)
Judicial review of Family Court processes (Bankside post)
Doyle's Guide top-ranked NZ family / relationship-property KC (2022, 2024, 2026 listings)
2022 NZ Lawyer power-list (42nd; only female KC in private practice listed)
practitioners.hook: Top-ranked Doyle's Guide family/relationship-property KC who has run judicial-review challenges to Family Court process — natural KC-level voice for procedural-middle view on how PA findings should be reviewed.
practitioners.notes: Specific public PA stance not publicly confirmed.

practitioners.name: Te Korowai Ture-a-Whānau (Independent Panel, 2018-2019)
practitioners.firm: Independent Panel examining the 2014 family justice reforms (Ministry of Justice, NZ)
named_cases: Te Korowai Ture-a-Whānau final report (June 2019) — 70 recommendations across child-centred processes, safety, inclusivity, delay, and access to services
Adopted as the NZ government's phased family-justice reform programme (2020-)
Beehive 2020 announcement — https://www.beehive.govt.nz/release/speeding-family-court-reducing-stress-families
Underlying empirical and stakeholder-submission record (Backbone, NCIWR, NZLS Family Law Section, NZ Bar Association)
practitioners.primary_contact: Ministry of Justice family-justice-reform team via https://www.justice.govt.nz/justice-sector-policy/key-initiatives/family-justice-reform/
practitioners.hook: Post-2019 government-adopted 'child-centred + safety-primary' doctrinal frame against which every NZ PA argument now has to be positioned — institutional reference equivalent to AU's Family Law Amendment Act 2023 backdrop.
practitioners.notes: Institutional entry; named panel members listed in the report appendices.

practitioners.name: NZLS Family Law Section
practitioners.firm: Family Law Section, New Zealand Law Society — Te Kāhui Ture o Aotearoa
bar_admissions: Institutional — specialist section of the national law society
named_cases: Regular submissions on Care of Children Act, Family Violence Act and family-justice reform
First Chair Margaret Casey KC (Auckland); subsequent Chairs include Anita Chan KC (Dunedin)
'Find a Family Lawyer' public-facing directory — https://www.familylaw.org.nz/public/find-a-lawyer/

Practice notes and guidelines for family-law practitioners (including lawyer-for-child) — https://www.lawsociety.org.nz/branches-sections-and-groups/family-law-section/guidelines-and-practice-notes/guidelines-and-protocol/
practitioners.hook: Single institutional gateway into the entire NZ family-law profession — every individual practitioner outreach in NZ ultimately routes through the Section's membership / directory.
practitioners.notes: Specific PA-policy position not publicly confirmed as a single stance document.

Dr Carolyn Henwood QC — could not verify publicly indexed primary-source family-law profile; known public work is Henwood Trust and Confidential Listening and Assistance Service.
Anne Steele — could not verify as a specifically family-law-named practitioner in NZ Bar / NZLS register.

NZ Family Court Judges who have authored PA decisions — should be listed in a separate practitioners-nz-judges.md.

stance_distribution.notes: 5 recognition = 3 NZ-substantive (James, Henaghan, Goldson) + 2 cross-jurisdictional pointers (Mort AU, Ludmer CA). 6 critique = Leonetti, Tolmie, MacLennan, Busch, Backbone Collective, NCIWR. 6 middle = Boshier, Chan KC, Kearns KC, Chambers KC, Te Korowai Ture-a-Whānau panel, NZLS Family Law Section. Critique-weighted because that is the dominant published voice in the NZ jurisdiction.
